vulnerabilities.description: JWT audience validation is opt-in via RequireAudienceValidation flag. If not explicitly enabled, tokens without audience claim or with invalid audience may be accepted depending on implementation details not visible in provided code.
vulnerabilities.fix: Enable audience validation by default or make it mandatory for production environments. Validate aud claim against allowed audiences in all cases.

vulnerabilities.description: OpenTelemetry propagator extracts trace context from user-controlled HTTP headers without explicit sanitization, potentially allowing trace ID injection or observability system pollution.
vulnerabilities.fix: Validate and sanitize trace header inputs before propagation, limit header size, and use allowlist for trace parent formats.

vulnerabilities.description: Cookie security attributes (Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite) are configurable rather than enforced. Production code could accidentally set Secure=false or HttpOnly=false.
vulnerabilities.fix: Enforce Secure=true and HttpOnly=true in production. Make SameSite=Lax or Strict mandatory with opt-out only for documented edge cases.

owasp_checks.detail: JWT claims include user_id, org_id, role, scopes for authorization. Token types distinguished (access vs refresh).
Argon2id for passwords (OWASP recommended), SHA-1 removed from SAML, 256-bit minimum JWT secrets, crypto/rand used.
No SQL injection patterns visible. Code uses parameterized patterns in visible implementations.
Security considerations documented in code. Defense in depth with cookie flags, token types, state parameters.
Security attributes are configurable rather than enforced. Could be misconfigured in production (Secure flag, audience validation).
Strong password hashing (Argon2id), proper JWT implementation with expiration, state parameter for OAuth/OIDC CSRF protection.
SAML signature validation with SHA-256+, PKCE for OIDC, HMAC for state verification.

critical_fixes: Enforce Secure and HttpOnly cookie flags in production builds
Make JWT audience validation mandatory by default
